text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found.. source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

This characteristic contributes significantly to the overall safety of the devices in a clinical context. Potential hazards identified in the RMR were analyzed for effective mitigation measures, which were then implemented via system design during product development, including product labeling. The RMR and its appendix/appendices and associated documentation describe the hazards, use cases, potential harms, pre- and post- mitigation hazard frequency, severity, and overall risk level, and Risk Control Measures (RCMs). The table(s) also provide(s) system requirement specifications related to the risks or hazards, and test case IDs for verification and validations testing. title: 5.0 Clinical Evaluation Inputs and Data
text_content: Clinical data will be collected and assessed for information about the safety and efficacy of the HAL treatment platform during clinical use. The clinical evaluation process will include: • Identification of pertinent inputs and data • Appraisal of the data for relevance to the general safety and performance requirements • Analysis of the data to reach conclusions regarding the device performance, safety, and benefit-to-risk profile. The conclusions will be used as input for post-market clinical follow-up study design, and as feedback to the risk analysis process.

Project-specific requirements and timing may be specified in the Regulatory Plan and the Design and Development Plan. Regulations applicable to Varian Products 1.3 The product-specific standards, laws, and/or directives; and guidance documents listed within this record are used to demonstrate product compliance and/or conformity. source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 7.0 Verification Activities > 7.1 Risk control measures
text_content: The three risk control options shall be considered in their order of priority: o Inherently safe design and manufacture; o Protective measures in the device itself or in the manufacturing process; o Information for safety (warnings/precautions/contra-indications) and, where appropriate, training to users. All recognized risk control measures shall be implemented to reduce risk to or maintain risk at an acceptable level. Implementation of risk control measures shall continue until successive risk control options or measures do not further reduce the estimated risk. Whenever possible, requirements of international standards or their corresponding harmonized versions shall be utilized as risk control measures. These applied standards are listed or referenced in Appendix B of this RMP. The implementation of risk control measures shall be verified. The effectiveness of the implemented risk control measures shall be verified or, as appropriate, validated.

source: D65887 HAL4.0 IEC 60601-2-1 (2020) 201.10.101.1.1.5 - TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION by DOSE MONITORING SYSTEM Rev 01.docx.pdf
title: 4. IEC Compliance Checks
text_content: Compliance is checked as follows: a) b) c) e) TYPE TEST grade A – Statement regarding the means used to ensure compliance. Ref a) and c) - Grade A Type Tests – Design Statement The ability of the secondary channel, that is the BGM FPGA level, to terminate irradiation is verified between treatments as part of the dosimetry test during the mode (radiation type and energy) selection process which is called the CalCheck cycle. The CalCheck cycle includes a first check that the continuity of the cables for all ion chamber plate signals is verified. This continuity check is achieved by introducing a step change in the ion chamber power supply voltage level and then detecting the corresponding induced charges in the ion chamber collection plates. This is needed because later the dosimetry check is performed locally on the BGM-CONT only. Such a test is valid because passing the continuity test can prove that the cable connection and the connections through the ion chamber, are all intact.

text_content: Varian utilizes a process based on the Rational Unified Process, and is defined in document reference DND01 Design and Development. This procedure defines the phases of a project, and more specifically, the various documentation requirements for each project phase to provide a high level of confidence in both the product, and the processes used to develop that product. The procedure further defines the level of management oversight necessary, in the form of Phase Gates at the end of each prescribed phase. The main purpose of the procedure is to comply with global regulatory requirements for design control, in particular US FDA 21 CFR 820, and ISO 13485. A second document, RSK01 Medical Device Risk Management is used to supplement DND01 and provides guidance on Risk Management consistent with EN ISO 14971:2019 Error! Reference source not found..
